brand.title: Scientific Control Desk
brand.caption: Central navigation for apps, jobs and account access.

navHints.profile: Manage your personal profile and password
navHints.apps: Visible scientific apps for your current session
navHints.groups: Manage scientific team groups and their permissions
navHints.users: Manage user accounts and group memberships

hero.title: Designing Scientific Workflows with Clarity
hero.copy: Enter your scientific workspace to orchestrate calculations, monitor distributed jobs and manage secure access controls from a single command surface.

panel.title: Scientific Access
panel.copy: Sign in to load your role, group access, app visibility rules and personalized workspace.

header.title: Profile
header.subtitle: Update your identity information. These changes are available for user, admin and root roles.

errors.mustBeSignedIn: You must be signed in to update your profile.
errors.passwordMismatch: Password confirmation does not match the new password.

header.title: Scientific Apps
header.subtitle: Access each module from one place and scale your scientific pipeline over time.

header.title: Acid-Base Equilibrium Fractions
header.subtitle: Configure pKa values and pH mode to calculate the same fraction table produced by the legacy desktop app.

form.title: Data Entry
form.speciesSettingsOptional: Optional. Leave blank to use the model standard configuration.
form.initialCharge: Charge of the most protonated species

batch.description: Upload a CSV file with multiple compounds to calculate species fractions at one fixed pH value.
batch.studentNote: Students usually estimate pKa values outside the platform and then upload the final curated table here.
batch.thresholdHint: Only species with fraction greater than or equal to this value will remain in the final table.
batch.noRows: No species met the selected threshold for this file.

result.title: Results
result.submitting: Submitting molar fractions job to backend...
result.noRows: No final rows available yet for this historical job.

chart.title: Fraction curves
chart.subtitle: Each species is drawn as a curve across the computed pH range.

history.empty: No historical jobs yet for molar fractions.

header.title: Easy-rate Calculator
header.subtitle: Upload Gaussian log files for your reaction structures and compute rate constants with statistical mechanics, tunneling correction, and optional diffusion control.

form.title: Input Files
form.selectExecutionHint: Select one parsed execution to continue.

header.title: Marcus Rate Calculator
header.subtitle: Upload six Gaussian log files (reactants, adiabatic products, vertical products) to compute Marcus energies, reorganization energy, barriers, and rate constants.

header.title: Combinatorial Structure Designer
header.subtitle: Inspect scaffolds, assign substitution blocks and generate derivative libraries from SMILES notation.

step1.title: 1. Principal molecule and scaffold preview
step1.description: Define the principal SMILES, inspect it visually, and then choose the substitution sites that later will be assigned to your block molecules.

inspection.clickTip: Tip: click directly on the molecule image to select atoms with the mouse.

step2.title: 2. Catalog of substituents
step2.catalogNote: Catalog creation and edition are isolated in the SMILES catalog studio to keep the main workflow clean.
step2.browseHint: Browse by group, inspect the structure preview, and open the full detail modal on click.
step2.noCatalogMolecules: No catalog molecules available for the selected group.

step3.title: 3. Block assignment by location
step3.panelMinimized: Right panel minimized. Use "Expand right panel" to continue editing details.
step3.createFirstBlock: Create the first block to define which substituent molecules will be available.
step3.substLibHint: Browse by group and add a substituent directly to this block.

step4.title: 4. Continuous generation and outputs
step4.description: Configure generation, run continuously, and inspect/export resulting structures.
step4.setupHint: Run generation only after block molecules and scaffold positions are fully mapped.
step4.previousJobsHint: Open a historical run to inspect the structures currently rendered below.
step4.currentRunDisplayed: The current run is already displayed below. This table only lists older jobs.
step4.downloadZipTitle: Download all enumerated structures as ZIP. SVG files + one TXT with principal SMILES first and derivative SMILES after.
step4.preparingZipHint: Generating all derivative SVGs on-demand for ZIP export. This may take a while.
step4.noStructures: No generated structures available in this snapshot.
step4.inspectionHint: Expand this section to review molecular depictions and SMILES.

principalEditor.label: Principal SMILES
principalEditor.sketchHint: Use the sketcher below to edit the principal molecule.

siteSelection.title: Select substitution sites on the scaffold
siteSelection.hint: Click atoms in the visual preview to mark where later substitutions may be placed.
siteSelection.blockMappingHint: Create blocks first, then map these selected sites to the block that should own them.

patterns.title: Structural patterns
patterns.hint: Open the pattern modal to create SMARTS rules and toggle contour highlights on the principal molecule.
patterns.modalHint: Patterns are enabled by default. Toggle each one to show or hide its highlights and labels on the principal molecule.
patterns.howItWorks1: 1. Enabled patterns illuminate matching atoms on the principal molecule.
patterns.howItWorks2: 2. Pattern names are rendered over the highlighted area in the scaffold preview.
patterns.howItWorks3: 3. Click any pattern name below to open complete SMARTS details.

catalogStudio.title: SMILES catalog studio
catalogStudio.hint: Create or edit substituents without mixing this flow with block mapping.

catalogForm.step1Hint: A default name is prepared for each new draft. Replace it if you need a custom label.
catalogForm.step5Hint: Keep a short traceable source for this substituent entry.
catalogForm.emptySmartHint: Enter a SMILES string to unlock the live structure preview.

catalogSketcher.title: Catalog SMILES sketcher
catalogSketcher.hint: Draw the substituent in Ketcher and apply it to the current SMILES draft.

blocks.noSubstituentsYet: No substituents added to this block yet.
blocks.noAutoMatched: No catalog molecules auto-matched by the selected functional categories.
blocks.noCatalogMolecules: No catalog molecules available for the selected group.
blocks.substLibHint: Browse by group and add a substituent directly to this block.
blocks.assignPositions: Assign this block to scaffold positions
blocks.noAtomsHint: Inspect the principal molecule and select target atoms before assigning this block to positions.
blocks.noPositionsAssigned: This block still has no scaffold positions assigned. Choose the sites that should receive one of this block's molecules.

header.title: Identity and Access
header.subtitle: Manage users, groups, memberships and app-level RBAC in a consistent workspace.

users.title: Users
users.description: User actions are role-aware. Admins can only manage users in their managed groups.

createUser.title: Create user
createUser.hint: Define username, role and primary group for a new account.

createGroup.title: Create group
createGroup.hint: Groups represent scientific teams or environments.
createGroup.name: Group name

addMembership.title: Add membership
addMembership.hint: Map users into groups before assigning app-level rules.

permissions.title: App permissions
permissions.hint: Evaluated by group first, then user-level overrides.

groupConfig.title: Group app config
groupConfig.hint: Store app-specific defaults at group level. Users can still override per app.

header.title: Group Management
header.subtitle: Manage scientific groups, their members, and app-level access rules.

createGroup.title: Create group
createGroup.hint: Groups represent scientific teams or environments.
createGroup.name: Group name

header.title: User Management
header.subtitle: Manage user accounts, roles, and group memberships.

users.title: Users
users.description: Admins can only manage users in their groups.

createUser.title: Create user
createUser.hint: Admins can create accounts only for their groups.

addMembership.title: Add membership
addMembership.hint: Assign an existing user to a group with a specific role.

smilesBatchInput.label: SMILES batch input
smilesBatchInput.hint: Supports plain SMILES lists, .smi/.txt lists, and CSV files where column 1 is name and column 2 is SMILES.
smilesBatchInput.drawSmilesTitle: Draw a molecule with Ketcher and add it to the list
smilesBatchInput.uploadFileTitle: Upload a .smi, .txt or .csv file with plain SMILES or name/SMILES pairs

hero.title: CADMAPy
hero.desc: Score candidate molecules against a disease-specific reference drug family using ADMET properties, toxicity and synthetic accessibility — prioritising the best candidates for synthesis and further evaluation.
hero.step1: Select a disease baseline from the pre-built reference sets (Neuro or RETT) or create a custom family.
hero.step2: Upload candidate CSVs exported from Smile-it, Toxicity Properties and SA Score, or a single combined CSV.
hero.step3: Rank and compare candidates by selection score against the reference band — export the results as CSV.

step1.title: Reference family
step1.desc: Choose the disease baseline against which candidates will be scored.
step1.noFamilies: No reference families available. Create one below to get started.
step1.traceabilityPlaceholder: Brief notes on compound selection criteria…
step1.uploadHint: Upload one or many CSV / SMI files, review headers and dead lines, and map the SMILES and optional metric columns before saving the family.

step2.title: Upload candidate files
step2.desc: Load your molecular data generated by Smile-it, Toxicity Properties and SA Score.
step2.quickFillTitle: Fast fill from the current candidate guide or previous runs
step2.quickFillDesc: If the current candidate file already contains names and SMILES, CADMA can launch the missing SA Score and Toxicity jobs for you. You can also reuse completed jobs below.
step2.quickFillReady: The imported guide preserves the candidate name label so the CADMA ranking stays fully identified.
step2.guideCombined: A combined CSV with all columns
step2.labelHelp: Reference family name identifies the baseline. The project label names this candidate batch, while molecule names are taken from the imported name column.
step2.quickFillGuideMissing: Upload a candidate guide with names and SMILES to enable automatic predictions.
step2.quickFillNamesFallback: Names will fall back to SMILES
step2.draftHint: You can pause once the project label and main SMILES guide are ready, then return later to add the remaining CSV files.

step3.title: Selection scores
step3.barHint: Bar length = selection score (ADME + toxicity + SA). Higher is better. Red dashed line = reference baseline.
step3.shadedHint: Shaded region = reference mean ± std dev. Points inside the band pass this metric.

history.empty: No previous CADMA Py executions yet.
history.title: Previous executions

familyDetail.copyTitle: Copy this family into your own editable workspace
familyDetail.name: Name *
familyDetail.readOnlyGuidance: Selecting the family keeps the original creator; copying creates your own editable version.
familyDetail.nullWarning: Some metrics have null values — compounds missing that field are excluded from its average.

importer.fallbackNote: Fallback names will use {{ prefix }} + counter.
importer.mappingHint: Use the combobox in each table header to tell the app what every column means.
